content: Quality measure validity requires: (1) Evidence link between measure and outcomes - HEDIS measures backed by systematic reviews; (2) Risk adjustment adequacy - APR-DRG, Elixhauser comorbidity indices explain 60-70% of mortality variation; (3) Measurement reliability - sufficient sample sizes for stable estimates (≥30 cases/year minimum); (4) Attribution accuracy - assigning outcomes to responsible providers (complicated by care fragmentation); (5) Gaming detection - monitoring for upcoding, cherry-picking patients, or neglecting unmeasured aspects. Composite measures (e.g., CMS Star Ratings) weight components but may mask specific weaknesses. Patient-reported outcome measures (PROMs) increasingly used but response bias and case-mix differences challenge comparisons.
